mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns during rate cuts are statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate above 4.5 percent signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales indicates robust consumer spending that supports corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative information coefficient over any 2-year rolling window or fails to outperform a single-proxy baseline by more than 0.05 IC.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in the index of industrial production during a late-cycle expansion signal capacity constraints and slowing output that compress equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in over 60 percent of late-cycle periods when INDPRO declines, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.05) outside late-cycle regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rates exceeding 2.0 percent trigger immediate portfolio rebalancing away from equities toward fixed income, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50 percent of observations when FEDFUNDS crosses above 2.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms a tightening labor market that boosts consumer confidence and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction term yields a negative out-of-sample R-squared over a 3-year walk-forward test or underperforms independent proxy signals by more than 0.08 IC.

mechanism_narrative: Surges in housing starts during a post-shock recovery phase indicate renewed capital investment and economic stabilization that generate positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Housing starts spikes produce negative or flat 20-day equity returns in post-shock environments, or the signal predictive power vanishes when tested outside recovery regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the index of industrial production reflect strengthening manufacturing activity that supports near-term equity price appreciation over the following week.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 over any 24-month rolling window or 5-day forward returns show no statistical relationship with INDPRO growth (p > 0.10).
